title: DOE Announces $1.67 Billion to Montana Renewables to Significantly Expand US Sustainable Aviation Fuel Production
content: The Department of Energyâ€™s Loan Programs Office has closed a $1.67 billion loan to Montana Renewables, LLC (MRL) to expand its Great Falls, Montana facility for sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), renewable diesel, and renewable naphtha production. The project will boost capacity from 140 million to 315 million gallons per year, with SAF as the primary output, positioning MRL as a leading global SAF producerâ€”expected to supply 50% of North Americaâ€™s SAF and 12% globally through 2030. The loan supports the Biden-Harris Administrationâ€™s SAF Grand Challenge, which targets 3 billion gallons of SAF by 2030 and 35 billion by 2050, aiding aviation decarbonization as the sector accounts for 11% of U.S. transportation emissions. The facility will use vegetable oils, fats, and greases for lower-carbon fuel production.
author: Tarana Ahmad
